meaning: v.閲嶆柊鍙戝姩,閲嶆柊鍙戝皠
examples: He is hoping to relaunch his film career with a remake of the 1971 British thriller.
They plan to relaunch it as a stand-alone company.
Users can close and relaunch task pages as required.
This is something the creators hope to combat when they relaunch the key later in 2013.
They now want to relaunch a peace process that has been stalled for four years.
